reforms.description: In 2002, the central government grant to universities was restructured to include components based on the number of graduations and research and teaching indicators, making funding more performance-oriented.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Performance-oriented funding is expected to enhance the quality of research and education, thereby supporting innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been in force since its introduction in 2002.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in how university funding is allocated, focusing on performance metrics.
reforms.source_quote: From 2002, the central government grant has three components: a grant based on the number of graduations; grants based on research and teaching indicators; and a block grant irrespective of the universities’ activities and results.

reforms.description: Norway implemented a targeted tax credit system for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in 2002 to support research and development (R&D), replacing a previous grant system, allowing SMEs to claim a tax credit of 20% on R&D expenses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit incentivizes SMEs to invest in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit system has been operational since its introduction in 2002.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly alters the support mechanism for R&D in SMEs, promoting innovation through fiscal incentives.
reforms.source_quote: A targeted tax credit system has recently been implemented, replacing a grant system that was set up in 2001.
